title: Add lesson picker to activate_next_bet
ADR-0016: undeniable quality contract
effect: Lock the quality bar (type/lint/format/test/hook/CI/schema/docs/reproducibility/dep-shape) as a versioned contract before any feature work resumes
notes: Paper-only slice. Documents the 9 quality gates (Q1..Q9) + hook policy (HARD; --no-verify reserved for operator) + coverage threshold (85% repo-wide, per-package overrides allowed). HITL.

title: Q1: mypy --strict per Python package as commit gate
effect: No untyped or partially-typed Python lands; mypyc-readiness benefits inherited at no extra cost
notes: mypy already pinned at 1.20.1 via mypyc venv; expose as ./repo.sh mypy. Per-package strict-mode opt-in via [tool.mypy.overrides]. Closeout check on commands facet. Blocked by ADR-0016 (quality-adr-0016) for the policy reference.

title: Q2: ruff (lint+format, S security rules) + shellcheck + shfmt as commit gates
effect: Style/correctness/security drift in Python and shell is impossible to land; formatter is canonical
notes: Pin ruff + shellcheck + shfmt as bootstrap tools. ruff config in pyproject.toml; enable: E, F, W, I (imports), B (bugbear), S (security), N (naming), C4 (comprehensions), SIM, PERF, RUF. shellcheck + shfmt -i 2 -ci.

title: Q3: unittest + coverage.py >=85% repo-wide + tools/test_base.py reusable base class
effect: Every Python branch is hit by a test; new tests are easier to author through one explicit base class (no magic, regular code)
notes: Stay on stdlib unittest (operator decision). Add tools/test_base.py — small extendible base class with: tmpdir fixture helper, mock-SSH/mock-urlopen helpers, assert_no_hardcoded_gh_literals helper. Pure regular code, no magic, no metaclass tricks. Coverage 85% repo-wide; per-package overrides up to 95% (e.g. infra_pkg) or down to 70% (dispatcher shims) declared in pyproject.toml [tool.coverage.report] section_thresholds.

title: Q4: HARD pre-commit + pre-push hooks (mypy + ruff + shellcheck + fast tests)
effect: No bad commit can land without explicit operator override; bypass policy is documented, not assumed
notes: HARD policy: hooks block commit on failure. Operator may use --no-verify; agents NEVER may. AGENTS.md gets a NEVER-list entry. Pre-commit fast subset: ruff format --check, ruff check, mypy on changed files, fast unit tests for changed packages. Pre-push: full agent_check + full mypy + full tests. Blocked by Q1 + Q2 + Q3.

title: Q5: GitHub Actions CI — matrix x86_64 + aarch64 — runs full quality + tests + smokes on every PR + push to main
effect: Same gates apply on host and CI; nothing reaches main that hasn't passed both arches
notes: GH Actions matrix: ubuntu-latest (x86_64) + ubuntu-24.04-arm (aarch64) native runners. Each runs ./repo.sh true + agent_check + mypy + ruff + shellcheck + test --coverage + zig-smoke + pyext smoke. Reproducibility: bootstrap stamp sha verified across two runs. Blocked by Q1..Q4.

title: Q6: JSON Schemas for facet.json + .agents/repo.json + pyext.toml + ideas.jsonl rows + targets.jsonl rows
effect: Schema drift is impossible — every config file is validated against a committed JSON Schema, not just by code in tools/facets.py
notes: JSON Schema 2020-12. agent_check validates each instance against its schema before any other checks run. Schemas live under schemas/ at repo root. Ideas + targets schemas codify the field set used by tools/ideas.py + tools/targets.py.

title: Q7: markdown link check + ADR-coverage check (every verb maps to an ADR or has explicit waiver)
effect: No dangling links in repo docs; no public verb ships without a documented decision behind it
notes: Markdown link check: every relative link inside repo docs must resolve to an existing file/anchor. ADR-coverage: every command in commands.facet declares either an ADR ref or `no_adr: 'rationale here'` in its facet.json entry. Waiver list reviewed quarterly.

title: Q8: ./repo.sh verify-reproducibility — re-runs each pinned tool's bootstrap and compares stamps + per-tool sha256s
effect: Pinned-tool integrity is checked, not assumed; cache rot or sha drift fails loud
notes: Re-fetches each tool's tarball, verifies sha256, reproduces the install layout, diff-checks the resulting stamp set. Goes beyond agent_check (which validates docs/routing). Wired into CI via Q5.

title: Q9: dep-shape verifier — every Python runtime dep is pinned-wheel-with-hash or source-built; no rogue PyPI fetches at runtime
effect: No surprise PyPI dependency lands by accident; the librt-musllinux-detection class of bugs is impossible to reintroduce
notes: Walks bootstrap/tools/*.sh and any pyext.toml; for each declared Python dep, asserts it's pinned with a sha256 OR source-built via a sibling bootstrap/tools/<dep>.sh. Fails on any TOOL_DEPS that includes Python but doesn't pin every transitive dep. Catches the gap that bit us with librt and packaging.
